items.summary: Oil futures rose on Wednesday as traders awaited what could be a historic release of emergency government reserves to counter the disruption to supplies from the attack on Iran.

items.headline: Microsoft backs Anthropic in Pentagon blacklist battle, urges temporary restraining order
items.summary: Microsoft threw its support behind Anthropic and advocated for a temporary restraining order to the Pentagon's supply chain risk designation.

items.headline: Powering AI: Europe switches on its first microgrid-connected data center
items.summary: AVK and Pure Data Centers’ microgrid facility in Ireland could mark the region’s first step toward a privately powered ecosystem.

items.headline: ‘Cruising used to feel special.’ Cruise lines were struggling even before the Iran conflict.
items.summary: Cruise stocks were among the S&P 500’s biggest decliners since the Middle East conflict, and it’s not just about rising fuel prices

items.summary: ”I have $560,000 in my IRA, $125,000 in stocks and $50,000 in savings.”

items.headline: Why European Central Bank officials are weighing hiking interest rates into a supply shock
items.summary: Traders increased bets on a possible interest rate rise in the eurozone this year after officials on Wednesday said the bloc’s central bank may be forced to act if the Iran war risks an inflation spike.

items.headline: German defense giant stock that’s surged has run out of gas
items.summary: Rheinmetall came up short of analysts expectaions for growth, net income and operating margins. Third Bridge warns its ambitious growth plans will present difficulties in execution

items.headline: Senators call for investigation into release, redaction of Epstein files

items.headline: Three cargo ships struck off Iran's coast, UK says, including one in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: The incidents mark the latest in a string of reported attacks in or near to the Strait of Hormuz, where shipping traffic has virtually ground to a halt.

items.headline: More pain in private credit as JPMorgan reportedly tightening lending while a $33 bln fund sees heavy redemptions
items.summary: The troubled private-credit industry was under further pressure after a report that JPMorgan Chase was tightening its lending criteria while a $33 billion fund was reported to have seen heavy withdrawals.

items.headline: CNBC Daily Open: Oil jitters persist as U.S. targets Iranian ships near Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Oil was slightly higher as traders expect a group of countries to tap emergency crude reserves to mitigate disruption caused by the war in the Middle East.

items.headline: Rheinmetall sees sales growth of up to 45% in 2026, says it's in 'prime position' to arm the U.S. amid war in Iran
items.summary: Arms maker Rheinmetall reported full-year sales that grew 29% year-over-year and said revenue would grow by even more this year.

items.headline: Treasury yields are higher as investors await key inflation report
items.summary: U.S. Treasury yields moved higher on Wednesday as investors awaited February's inflation report and monitored developments on the US-Iran war front.

items.headline: Travelers are turning to AI to plan trips — but hallucinations and trust gaps remain

items.headline: Asia-Pacific markets mostly higher as investors weigh developments in the Middle East
items.summary: Asia-Pacific markets traded mostly higher on Wednesday as investors assessed the ongoing Middle East war.

items.headline: India-China reset? Relaxed rules allow Beijing to invest in India after about six years of friction
items.summary: The Indian cabinet has approved changes to its foreign direct investment policy, allowing investments from "Land Bordering Countries."

items.headline: Iran sends millions of oil barrels to China through Strait of Hormuz even as war chokes the waterway

items.headline: How the Iran war and rising energy prices are threatening semiconductor demand

items.headline: What’s next for crude-oil prices as wealthy nations consider a release of their reserves
